description: national monument in California
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: US government agency established to handle the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II
description_wiki: The War Relocation Authority (WRA) was a United States government agency established to handle the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II.  It also operated the Fort Ontario Emergency Refugee Shelter in Oswego, New York, which was the only refugee camp set up in the United States for refugees from Europe.

surface_forms: Loomis, situated at the time an hour drive north of Sacramento, California
description: incorporated town in Placer County, California, United States
description_wiki: Loomis (formerly Pine, Pino, Smithville, and Placer) is an incorporated town in Placer County, California, United States.

description: capital city of the U.S. state of California and seat of Sacramento County
description_wiki: Sacramento is the capital city of the U.S. state of California.

description: city and the county seat of Placer County, California, United States
description_wiki: Auburn is a city in and the county seat of Placer County, California, United States.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

surface_forms: Japan bombed the U.S Navy in Pearl Harbor
description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Virginia, officially the Commonwealth of Virginia, is a state in the Southeastern and Mid-Atlantic regions of the United States between the Atlantic Coast and the Appalachian Mountains.

description: Japanese people who were the first generation of immigrants from Japan
description_wiki: Issei (一世, "first generation") are Japanese immigrants to countries in North America and South America.

entity: Block 45, Tule Lake War Relocation Center
description: national monument in California
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

surface_forms: the book The Train to Crystal City

description: Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of the United States from 1953 to 1969 (1891–1974)
description_wiki: Earl Warren (March 19, 1891 – July 9, 1974) was an American attorney and politician who served as the 30th governor of California from 1943 to 1953, and as the 14th chief justice of the United States from 1953 to 1969.

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Alameda (  AL-ə-MEE-də; Spanish: [alaˈmeða]; Spanish for "tree-lined path") is a city in Alameda County, California, United States, located in the East Bay region of the Bay Area.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: 1942 U.S. presidential order for the internment of Japanese-Americans
description_wiki: Executive Order 9066 was a United States presidential executive order signed and issued during World War II by United States president Franklin D. Roosevelt on February 19, 1942.

aliases: Crystal City Alien Enemy Detention Facility
description: building in Crystal City, Zavala County, Texas
description_wiki: Crystal City Internment Camp, located near Crystal City, Texas, was a place of confinement for people of Japanese, German, and Italian descent during World War II, and has been variously described as a detention facility or a concentration camp.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.
